title: Ποιμὴν παίζων
source: Émile Chambry, Ésope: Fables, texte établi et traduit (Les Belles Lettres, Paris, 1927), fable 318 (= Perry 210). Text as printed, including Chambry's editorial punctuation (« »). Pronunciation: reconstructed Attic, after W. S. Allen, Vox Graeca. Romanization: standard classical transliteration (ē/ō; th/ph/ch; y for υ, u in diphthongs; h = rough breathing; ēi/āi/ōi for iota subscript; accents omitted).

sentences.t: Ποιμὴν ἐξελαύνων αὐτοῦ τὴν ποίμνην ἀπό τινος κώμης πορρωτέρω, διετέλει τοιαύτῃ παιδιᾷ χρώμενος·
sentences.tr: Poimēn exelaunōn autou tēn poimnēn apo tinos kōmēs porrōterō, dietelei toiautēi paidiāi chrōmenos:
sentences.l: A shepherd, driving-out his flock from a certain village rather-far, kept-on such a game practising:
sentences.i: A shepherd who drove his flock some way out from the village kept up this standing joke:
sentences.n: διετέλει + participle (χρώμενος) = 'kept on doing' — verbs of continuing take a supplementary participle, not an infinitive. παιδιά is 'play, joke' — one accent away from παιδία 'children.' The raised dot · at the end is the Greek semicolon: the joke itself follows.

sentences.t: ἐπιβοώμενος γὰρ τοὺς κωμήτας ἐπὶ βοήθειαν, ἔλεγεν ὡς λύκοι τοῖς προβάτοις ἐπῆλθον.
sentences.tr: epiboōmenos gar tous kōmētas epi boētheian, elegen hōs lykoi tois probatois epēlthon.
sentences.l: for, calling-out-to the villagers for help, he-kept-saying that wolves upon-the-sheep had-come.
sentences.i: he would call the villagers to the rescue, crying that wolves had attacked the sheep.
sentences.n: γάρ 'for' marks this as the explanation of the 'game.' The imperfect ἔλεγεν paints habitual, repeated action — he kept doing it. ἐπέρχομαι 'come upon, attack' takes the dative (τοῖς προβάτοις); ὡς rather than ὅτι flavors the report as his claim — which is false.

sentences.t: Δὶς δὲ καὶ τρὶς τῶν ἐκ τῆς κώμης ἐκπλαγέντων καὶ ἐκπηδησάντων, εἶτα μετὰ γέλωτος ἀπαλλαγέντων, συνέβη τὸ τελευταῖον τῇ ἀληθείᾳ λύκους ἐπελθεῖν.
sentences.tr: Dis de kai tris tōn ek tēs kōmēs ekplagentōn kai ekpēdēsantōn, eita meta gelōtos apallagentōn, synebē to teleutaion tēi alētheiāi lykous epelthein.
sentences.l: And twice and three-times, those from the village having-been-startled and having-rushed-out, then with laughter having-gone-away, it-happened at-the-last that in-truth wolves came-on.
sentences.i: Two or three times the villagers took fright and came running out, only to go home laughing — until at last wolves really did come.
sentences.n: A stacked genitive absolute: τῶν … ἐκπλαγέντων καὶ ἐκπηδησάντων … ἀπαλλαγέντων — a whole background scene packed into genitive participles before the main verb συνέβη arrives. You'll see the genitive absolute constantly. τῇ ἀληθείᾳ, 'in truth' = 'really': a dative used adverbially.

sentences.t: Ἀποτεμνομένων δὲ αὐτῶν τὴν ποίμνην καὶ τοῦ ποιμένος ἐπὶ βοηθείᾳ τοὺς κωμήτας ἐπικαλουμένου, ἐκεῖνοι ὑπολαβόντες αὐτὸν παίζειν κατὰ τὸ ἔθος, ἧττον ἐφρόντιζον·
sentences.tr: Apotemnomenōn de autōn tēn poimnēn kai tou poimenos epi boētheiāi tous kōmētas epikaloumenou, ekeinoi hypolabontes auton paizein kata to ethos, hētton ephrontizon:
sentences.l: And they cutting-up the flock, and the shepherd for help the villagers calling-upon, those-men, having-supposed him to-be-joking according-to habit, the-less cared:
sentences.i: While the wolves were tearing through the flock and the shepherd shouted to the villagers for help, they supposed he was joking as usual and paid little attention:
sentences.n: Two genitive absolutes back to back — the wolves at their work (ἀποτεμνομένων … αὐτῶν), the shepherd shouting (τοῦ ποιμένος … ἐπικαλουμένου) — before the villagers' reaction lands. The same construction as the previous sentence, now at the fatal moment. ἧττον ἐφρόντιζον, 'they cared the less': dry understatement.

sentences.t: οὕτως αὐτῷ συνέβη τῶν προβάτων στερηθῆναι.
sentences.tr: houtōs autōi synebē tōn probatōn sterēthēnai.
sentences.i: and so it came about that he lost his sheep.
sentences.n: συνέβη + dative + infinitive: 'it befell him to…'. Verbs of depriving take the genitive — hence τῶν προβάτων στερηθῆναι, 'to be robbed of the sheep.'

sentences.t: Ὁ λόγος δηλοῖ ὅτι τοῦτο κερδαίνουσιν οἱ ψευδολόγοι τὸ μηδὲ ὅταν ἀληθεύωσι, πιστεύεσθαι.
sentences.tr: Ho logos dēloi hoti touto kerdainousin hoi pseudologoi to mēde hotan alētheuōsi, pisteuesthai.
sentences.l: The story shows that this profit the false-speakers: the not-even when they-speak-truth, to-be-believed.
sentences.i: The story shows that what liars gain is this — not to be believed even when they tell the truth.
sentences.n: The house formula ὁ λόγος δηλοῖ ὅτι, 'the story shows that…' — you'll meet it at the end of most fables. The moral turns on a sardonic 'profit': τοῦτο κερδαίνουσιν … τὸ … πιστεύεσθαι, where the articular infinitive (τό + infinitive) turns 'not being believed' into the noun that is their prize. ἀληθεύωσι is subjunctive with ὅταν: 'whenever they do tell the truth.'

glossary.ἐξελαύνων: ἐξελαύνω — drive out; pres. part. masc. nom. sg.
glossary.τὴν: ὁ, ἡ, τό — the; fem. acc. sg.
glossary.τινος: τις — some, a certain; gen. sg.
glossary.πορρωτέρω: πόρρω — far off; comparative adverb: 'rather far'
glossary.διετέλει: διατελέω + part. — continue, keep on; impf. 3 sg.
glossary.παιδιᾷ: παιδιά — play, game, joke; dat. sg. (not παιδία 'children')
glossary.χρώμενος: χράομαι + dat. — use, practise; pres. part. masc. nom. sg.
glossary.ἐπιβοώμενος: ἐπιβοάομαι — call out to, summon by shouting; pres. part. masc. nom. sg.
glossary.ἐπὶ: ἐπί + acc./dat. — for, to (here: purpose, 'for help')
glossary.ἔλεγεν: λέγω — say; impf. 3 sg.: 'he kept saying'
glossary.ὡς: ὡς — that (reporting a claim); when, as
glossary.ἐπῆλθον: ἐπέρχομαι + dat. — come upon, attack; aor. 3 pl.
glossary.τῆς: ὁ, ἡ, τό — the; fem. gen. sg.
glossary.ἐκπλαγέντων: ἐκπλήττομαι — be startled, panic; aor. part. gen. pl. (genitive absolute)
glossary.ἐκπηδησάντων: ἐκπηδάω — leap out, rush out; aor. part. gen. pl.
glossary.ἀπαλλαγέντων: ἀπαλλάττομαι — go away, depart; aor. part. gen. pl.
glossary.συνέβη: συμβαίνω — happen, come to pass; aor. 3 sg.: 'it happened'
glossary.τὸ: ὁ, ἡ, τό — the; neut. sg.
glossary.τελευταῖον: τελευταῖος — last; τὸ τελευταῖον (adverbial): 'at the last, finally'
glossary.τῇ: ὁ, ἡ, τό — the; fem. dat. sg.
glossary.ἀληθείᾳ: ἀλήθεια — truth; dat. sg.; τῇ ἀληθείᾳ = 'in truth, really'
glossary.ἐπελθεῖν: ἐπέρχομαι — come upon, attack; aor. inf.
glossary.ἀποτεμνομένων: ἀποτέμνω — cut off, cut up, butcher; pres. mid./pass. part. gen. pl. (genitive absolute)
glossary.αὐτῶν: αὐτός — they; gen. pl. (here: the wolves)
glossary.ἐπικαλουμένου: ἐπικαλέομαι — call upon, appeal to; pres. part. masc. gen. sg.
glossary.ἐκεῖνοι: ἐκεῖνος — that one; masc. nom. pl.: 'those men'
glossary.ὑπολαβόντες: ὑπολαμβάνω — suppose, assume; aor. part. masc. nom. pl.
glossary.ἐφρόντιζον: φροντίζω — give heed, care; impf. 3 pl.
glossary.στερηθῆναι: στερέομαι + gen. — be deprived of, lose; aor. pass. inf.
glossary.ὁ: ὁ, ἡ, τό — the; masc. nom. sg.
glossary.λόγος: λόγος — story, tale; word, account; nom. sg.
glossary.δηλοῖ: δηλόω — show, make clear; pres. 3 sg.
glossary.ψευδολόγοι: ψευδολόγος — speaker of falsehoods, liar; nom. pl.
glossary.ἀληθεύωσι: ἀληθεύω — speak the truth; pres. subj. 3 pl.
glossary.πιστεύεσθαι: πιστεύω — believe, trust; pres. pass. inf.: 'to be believed'
